subtasks.description: Scan IAM policies from AWS IAM database to extract all active policies, roles, users, groups, and their permission configurations
Query CloudTrail logs to identify all authentication events, permission changes, and access patterns over the audit period
Evaluate IAM policies and CloudTrail activity against security policy rules (least privilege, MFA enforcement, excessive permissions, suspicious activities) and identify violations
Generate comprehensive compliance report synthesizing policy violations, risk scores, remediation recommendations, and executive summary with audit findings

plan.cost_rationale: Decomposed into 4 subtasks: (1) IAM policy extraction via SQL (mid-tier: multi-table join across IAM entities) and (2) CloudTrail log analysis via SQL (mid-tier: complex filtering and aggregation of log events) execute in parallel. These are two independent data sources requiring separate queries. (3) Policy violation checking (frontier-tier: requires evaluating multiple policy rules against both datasets with exception handling and risk assessment logic) depends on both data sources. (4) Compliance report generation (frontier-tier: requires cross-source reconciliation, classification of findings, risk prioritization, and narrative synthesis) depends on violation results. This is the minimum viable decomposition; combining IAM+CloudTrail into one query would be suboptimal as they query separate systems; compliance report generation is cross_recon (not just output formatting) because it reconciles violation signals into unified posture assessment.

output.sql: SELECT log_id, user_id, permission, resource, access_time, action, result FROM access_log_facts ORDER BY access_time DESC

SELECT log_id, user_id, permission, resource, access_time, action, result, DATE_TRUNC('day', access_time) AS access_date, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id, DATE_TRUNC('day', access_time)) AS daily_action_count, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id, permission) AS permission_usage_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN result = 'DENY' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS total_denials, COUNT(CASE WHEN action ILIKE '%auth%' OR action ILIKE '%login%' OR action ILIKE '%signin%' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS auth_event_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN action ILIKE '%policy%' OR action ILIKE '%permission%' OR action ILIKE '%role%' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS permission_change_count, FIRST_VALUE(access_time) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY access_time ASC ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS first_seen, LAST_VALUE(access_time) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY access_time ASC ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS last_seen FROM access_log_facts ORDER BY access_time DESC

output.reason: Security audit workflow performs read-only analysis of IAM and CloudTrail logs to detect policy violations; no sensitive modifications or restricted actions are performed.
